title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Arthur Calgary confronts Kirsten (also referred to as Corston or Caulston) Lindström regarding the murder of Mrs. Agle. While Calgary previously provided an alibi for Jacqueline (Jacques), he now posits that Jacqueline was the mastermind who manipulated Kirsten into committing the murder. Calgary explains that Jacqueline exploited Kirsten's affection for him to convince her to kill Mrs. Agle between seven and seven-thirty to facilitate a theft. Kirsten eventually breaks down, admitting her love for Jacques but claiming his actions were evil rather than her own.

Calgary further accuses Kirsten of murdering Philip Darrent and stabbing Tina. He reveals that Tina’s cryptic phrase, "the cup is empty," indicated that Kirsten had stabbed Philip while pretending to bring him coffee, leaving an empty cup on the floor. Kirsten admits to the killings, driven by fear and a desire to protect herself after realizing Jacques had deceived her. Following the revelation, Mary Darlant attacks Kirsten in a rage before leaving the room in grief.

As the group processes the truth, Leo Aghart defends Kirsten’s character, suggesting her actions stemmed from suffering, while Gwendolyn remains resentful of the torment Kirsten caused. Calgary suggests that Kirsten will likely flee to London but will eventually be caught. The passage concludes with a discussion about Tina’s recovery and her relationship with Mickey. In a moment of relief and shifting emotions, Hester expresses a sudden desire to marry Calgary.
